hero.question: Will Illinois win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 17.2%, the market is pricing Illinois as a tournament winner once every 6 years, which undervalues a program that just landed the nation's best recruiting class and has tournament-caliber guards already in the system. The real edge is watching whether Terrence Shannon Jr. stays healthy and if the roster chemistry clicks by March, because if it does, 17% looks like free money for contrarians.

daily_take.headline: Illinois at 17.2% is the sleeper undervalue in March betting season.
daily_take.deck: Illinois has gone nowhere in 24 hours, but the market is pricing them like a mid-major. They've got a legitimate tournament roster, blue-chip recruiting class coming in, and Brad Underwood's system is finally clicking. At 17.2%, you're getting better than 5-to-1 odds on a program that will be in the conversation. The sharps know March rewards depth and defensive intensity, not just star power. This is a 22-25% market at worst.
